name: Thymalin
subtitle: Thymic Peptide Bioregulator | Immune Modulator

modifications: Contains short peptides KE, EW, EDP
molecular.halfLife: Not established (complex mixture); effects persist for weeks

overview: Thymalin is a natural peptide bioregulator derived from calf thymus gland, developed in the Soviet Union in the 1970s. It contains a mixture of thymic peptides that support immune restoration, anti-aging, and tissue regeneration. Research spanning decades has shown significant geroprotective effects, with studies demonstrating up to 2-fold reduction in mortality rates and 4-fold reduction when combined with Epithalamin.
mechanism: Thymalin's immunoprotective action stems from short peptides (KE, EW, EDP) that specifically bind to double-stranded DNA and histone proteins to regulate gene expression. It stimulates differentiation and activity of T-lymphocytes, especially CD4+ and CD8+ cells, promotes cytokine regulation, enhances antigen response, and stimulates stem cell differentiation. It also affects gerontogene activity and immune system protein synthesis.
keyBenefits: Potent immune system restoration and modulation
Reduced mortality rate in clinical studies (2.0-2.1 fold)
Normalized cardiovascular, endocrine, and nervous system function
Reduced acute respiratory disease incidence (2.0-2.4 fold)
Safe for elderly populations with no side effects

items.name: Immune Restoration
items.description: Stimulates T-lymphocyte differentiation and activity for comprehensive immune system restoration.

items.name: Lymphopenia Recovery
items.description: Accelerates recovery of lymphocyte populations including CD4+, CD3+HLA-DR+, B-cells, and NK-cells.

items.name: Respiratory Illness Prevention
items.description: 2.0-2.4 fold reduction in acute respiratory disease incidence in elderly patients.

items.name: Mortality Reduction
items.description: Clinical studies showed 2.0-2.1 fold mortality reduction over 6-8 years in treated elderly.

items.name: Geroprotection
items.description: Normalizes basic body functions and improves cardiovascular, endocrine, immune, and nervous systems.

items.name: Combined Protocol
items.description: When combined with Epithalamin for 6 years, mortality decreased 4.1 times vs control.

items.name: COVID-19 Support
items.description: Faster reversal of lymphopenia and immune marker recovery in severe COVID-19 patients.

items.name: Antitumor Effects
items.description: Tumor growth arrest and regression observed in over half of animals in sarcoma studies.

items.name: Thymus Normalization
items.description: Normalizes thymus function during aging process.

deliveryMethods.overview: Administered via intramuscular or subcutaneous injection in short 5-10 day cycles. Often combined with other bioregulators like Epitalon or Vilon for enhanced systemic effects. Practically no side effects, safe for elderly populations.

steps: Clean work area and hands thoroughly

keyBenefits: No side effects in elderly populations

interactions.notes: Combined use showed 4.1-fold mortality reduction vs 2.0-fold with Thymalin alone.
Often used together for enhanced systemic recovery and immune support.
Similar thymic origin; different mechanisms can complement each other.
No known negative interactions; different mechanisms.

timeline.effects: Improved immune markers on blood tests

references.title: The Use of Thymalin for Immunocorrection and Molecular Aspects of Biological Activity
references.keyFindings: Detailed molecular mechanisms of Thymalin's immunoprotective action through peptides KE, EW, EDP binding to DNA and histones.

references.title: Peptides of Pineal Gland and Thymus Prolong Human Life
references.keyFindings: 6-8 year study showed 2.0-2.1 fold mortality reduction with Thymalin; 4.1-fold when combined with Epithalamin.

references.title: Peptide Drug Thymalin Regulates Immune Status in Severe COVID-19 Older Patients
references.keyFindings: Faster lymphopenia reversal and improved recovery of CD4+, CD3+HLA-DR+, B-cells, and NK-cells in COVID-19.

references.title: Effect of Thymalin on Tumor and Thymus in Activation Therapy
references.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
references.keyFindings: Antitumor effects with tumor growth arrest in >50% of animals; growth suppressed by 78% in others.
